revision.comment: 出典を参考に作成
revision.text:
'''泉山七福神巡り'''（せんざんしちふくじんめぐり）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]の[[泉涌寺]]（せんにゅうじ）山内の7ヵ所<ref>番外を含めると9ヵ所となる。泉涌寺本坊も番外に含まれる</ref>の[[塔頭]]（山内寺院）で、毎年[[1月]]の第2[[月曜日]]（[[成人の日]]）に開催されている[[七福神]]めぐりの行事である<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 泉山七福神巡り]</ref><ref>成人の日と同様、[[1999年]]（[[平成11年]]）までは毎年[[1月15日]]に開催されていた。</ref>。
== 概要 == 七福神信仰は[[室町時代]]の京都が発祥とされ、それが次第に日本各地に拡がっていったといわれる。正月に参詣すると福がもたらされるという。泉山七福神巡りは[[1951年]]（[[昭和26年]]）より毎年成人の日に行われており、新春恒例の行事として年々盛大になっているとされる<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/event.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 年中行事]</ref>。当日は一番目の[[即成院]]にて授与される'''福笹'''<ref>常緑であり、垂直に伸びる[[ササ|笹]]は、運勢が上向きに上昇するという縁起がある。</ref>を持って、吉兆を表す[[縁起物]]を福笹につけながら塔頭を順に参拝していく人で、例年多くの人出がある。また、番外を含めた9ヵ所では七福神巡りの[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]を授与しており、これを福笹と合わせて集めて廻る人も少なくない<ref>朱印の授与については通年で行われている。</ref>。開催される時間は、朝8時より夕方16時頃までである。
== 泉山七福神巡りの一覧 == {| class="wikitable" ! 番号 !! 塔頭 !! 神 !! 縁起 !! 所在地 |- | 一番 || [[即成院]] || [[福禄寿]] || 長寿・幸福 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町28 |- | 二番 || [[戒光寺]] || [[弁財天]] || 商売繁盛・芸能上達 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町29 |- | 三番 || [[観音寺 (京都市東山区)|観音寺]] || [[えびす|恵比寿]] || 商業繁栄・海運守護 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町32 |- | 四番 || [[来迎院 (京都市東山区)|来迎院]] || [[布袋]]尊 || 知足福・家業繁盛 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町33 |- | 五番 || [[雲龍院]] || [[大黒天]] || 福徳 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町36 |- | 六番 || [[悲田院]] || [[毘沙門天]] || 財宝自在 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町35 |- | 七番 || [[法音院 (京都市東山区)|法音院]] || [[寿老人]] || 長寿・健康 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町30 |- | 番外 || [[新善光寺 (京都市東山区)|新善光寺]] || [[愛染明王]] || 敬愛和合 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町31 |- | 番外 || [[泉涌寺]]本坊 || [[泉涌寺#楊貴妃観音像|楊貴妃観音]] || 良縁・諸願成就 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町27 |}
== 脚注・出典 == {{Reflist}} *[http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~uy7m-ssk/miyako/sennyuji.pdf 京都泉涌寺七福神参拝御案内（PDF)]
== 外部リンク == * [http://blog.goo.ne.jp/mimoron/e/6870ed1030fec541992464c51850a71e 「泉山七福神巡り」。成人の日に「泉涌寺」の山内寺院を、福笹を持って巡る、年に1度の福集め。] * [http://kyotocf.com/matsuri/%E6%B3%89%E5%B1%B1%E4%B8%83%E7%A6%8F%E7%A5%9E%E5%B7%A1%E3%82%8A%E3%83%BB%E3%83%BB%E3%83%BB%E5%AE%9F%E3%81%AF%E4%B9%9D%E7%A6%8F%E7%A5%9E%E5%B7%A1%E3%82%8A/ 泉山七福神巡り・・・実は九福神巡り] * [http://kyotohotelsearch.com/blog/2010/04/29/sennyujishichifukujin/ 京都観光旅行のあれこれ 泉涌寺の七福神] * [http://4travel.jp/travelogue/10749091 泉涌寺七福神めぐり（泉山七福神めぐり） 2013]

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ [[泉涌寺道]]記述追加
revision.text:
'''泉山七福神巡り'''（せんざんしちふくじんめぐり）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]の[[泉涌寺]]（せんにゅうじ）山内の7ヵ所<ref>番外を含めると9ヵ所となる。泉涌寺本坊も番外に含まれる</ref>の[[塔頭]]（山内寺院）で、毎年[[1月]]の第2[[月曜日]]（[[成人の日]]）に開催されている[[七福神]]めぐりの行事である<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 泉山七福神巡り]</ref><ref>成人の日と同様、[[1999年]]（[[平成11年]]）までは毎年[[1月15日]]に開催されていた。</ref>。
== 概要 == 七福神信仰は[[室町時代]]の京都が発祥とされ、それが次第に日本各地に拡がっていったといわれる。正月に参詣すると福がもたらされるという。泉山七福神巡りは[[1951年]]（[[昭和26年]]）より毎年成人の日に行われており、新春恒例の行事として年々盛大になっているとされる<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/event.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 年中行事]</ref>。当日は一番目の[[即成院]]にて授与される'''福笹'''<ref>常緑であり、垂直に伸びる[[ササ|笹]]は、運勢が上向きに上昇するという縁起がある。</ref>を持って、吉兆を表す[[縁起物]]を福笹につけながら[[泉涌寺道]]沿道の塔頭を順に参拝していく人で、例年多くの人出がある。また、番外を含めた9ヵ所では七福神巡りの[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]を授与しており、これを福笹と合わせて集めて廻る人も少なくない<ref>朱印の授与については通年で行われている。</ref>。開催される時間は、朝8時より夕方16時頃までである。
== 泉山七福神巡りの一覧 == {| class="wikitable" ! 番号 !! 塔頭 !! 神 !! 縁起 !! 所在地 |- | 一番 || [[即成院]] || [[福禄寿]] || 長寿・幸福 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町28 |- | 二番 || [[戒光寺]] || [[弁財天]] || 商売繁盛・芸能上達 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町29 |- | 番外 || [[新善光寺 (京都市東山区)|新善光寺]] || [[愛染明王]] || 敬愛和合 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町31 |- | 三番 || [[観音寺 (京都市東山区)|観音寺]] || [[えびす|恵比寿]] || 商業繁栄・海運守護 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町32 |- | 四番 || [[来迎院 (京都市東山区)|来迎院]] || [[布袋]]尊 || 知足福・家業繁盛 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町33 |- | 五番 || [[雲龍院]] || [[大黒天]] || 福徳 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町36 |- | 番外 || [[泉涌寺]]本坊 || [[泉涌寺#楊貴妃観音像|楊貴妃観音]] || 良縁・諸願成就 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町27 |- | 六番 || [[悲田院]] || [[毘沙門天]] || 財宝自在 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町35 |- | 七番 || [[法音院 (京都市東山区)|法音院]] || [[寿老人]] || 長寿・健康 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町30 |}

'''泉山七福神巡り'''（せんざんしちふくじんめぐり）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]の[[泉涌寺]]（せんにゅうじ）山内の7ヵ所<ref>番外を含めると9ヵ所となる。泉涌寺本坊も番外に含まれる</ref>の[[塔頭]]（山内寺院）で、毎年[[1月]]の第2[[月曜日]]（[[成人の日]]）に開催されている[[七福神]]めぐりの行事である<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 泉山七福神巡り]</ref><ref>成人の日と同様、[[1999年]]（[[平成11年]]）までは毎年[[1月15日]]に開催されていた。</ref>。
== 概要 == 七福神信仰は[[室町時代]]の京都が発祥とされ、それが次第に日本各地に拡がっていったといわれる。正月に参詣すると福がもたらされるという。泉山七福神巡りは[[1951年]]（[[昭和26年]]）より毎年成人の日に行われており、新春恒例の行事として年々盛大になっているとされる<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php 御寺 泉涌寺 公式サイト 年中行事]（2016年12月28日時点の[[インターネット・アーカイブ|アーカイブ]]）</ref>。当日は一番目の[[即成院]]にて授与される'''福笹'''<ref>常緑であり、垂直に伸びる[[ササ|笹]]は、運勢が上向きに上昇するという縁起がある。</ref>を持って、吉兆を表す[[縁起物]]を福笹につけながら[[泉涌寺道]]沿道の塔頭を順に参拝していく人で、例年多くの人出がある。また、番外を含めた9ヵ所では七福神巡りの[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]を授与しており、これを福笹と合わせて集めて廻る人も少なくない<ref>朱印の授与については通年で行われている。</ref>。開催される時間は、朝8時より夕方16時頃までである。
== 泉山七福神巡りの一覧 == {| class="wikitable" ! 番号 !! 塔頭 !! 神 !! 縁起 !! 所在地 |- | 一番 || [[即成院]] || [[福禄寿]] || 長寿・幸福 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町28 |- | 二番 || [[戒光寺]] || [[弁財天]] || 商売繁盛・芸能上達 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町29 |- | 番外 || [[新善光寺 (京都市東山区)|新善光寺]] || [[愛染明王]] || 敬愛和合 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町31 |- | 三番 || [[観音寺 (京都市東山区)|観音寺]] || [[えびす|恵比寿]] || 商業繁栄・海運守護 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町32 |- | 四番 || [[来迎院 (京都市東山区)|来迎院]] || [[布袋]]尊 || 知足福・家業繁盛 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町33 |- | 五番 || [[雲龍院]] || [[大黒天]] || 福徳 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町36 |- | 番外 || [[泉涌寺]]本坊 || [[泉涌寺#楊貴妃観音像|楊貴妃観音]] || 良縁・諸願成就 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町27 |- | 六番 || [[悲田院]] || [[毘沙門天]] || 財宝自在 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町35 |- | 七番 || [[法音院 (京都市東山区)|法音院]] || [[寿老人]] || 長寿・健康 || 京都市東山区泉涌寺山内町30 |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|qq=|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|qq=|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
== List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites == {| class="wikitable" |+'''List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites''' |- ! Number !! Sub-temple !! Deity !! Blessings !! Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|qq=|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]] || Longevity and Happiness || Kyoto City, Higashiyama Ward, Sennyūji Sannai-cho 28 |- | Site 2 || {{ill|Kaikōji|ja|戒光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikōji|qq=|1=Kaikō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043593}} || [[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] [[Category:Events in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Tourism in Kyoto]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kyoto]] [[Category:Temples in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Temples of the Shingon Sennyu-ji branch]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q21653362}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
== List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites == {| class="wikitable" |+'''List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites''' |- ! Number !! Sub-temple !! Deity !! Blessings !! Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]] || Longevity revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
== List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites == {| class="wikitable" |+'''List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites''' |- ! Number !! Sub-temple !! Deity !! Blessings !! Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]] || Longevity and Happiness || Kyoto City, Higashiyama Ward, Sennyūji Sannai-cho 28 |- | Site 2 || {{ill|Kaikōji|ja|戒光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikōji|1=Kaikō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on (京都市東山区)|lt=Raikyōin|lt_ja=来迎院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raikyōin|1=Raigō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523759}} || {{ill|Hotei|ja|布袋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 布袋|1=Budai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q599838}} || Contentment and Prosperity in Family Business || (京都市東山区)|lt=Hoon-in|lt_ja=法音院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōon-in|1=Hōon-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55537255}} || [[:en:Jurōjin|Jurojin]] || Longevity and Health || Kyoto revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
== List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites == {| class="wikitable" |+'''List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites''' |- ! Number !! Sub-temple !! Deity !! Blessings !! Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || [[:en:Fukurokuju|Fukurokuju]] || Longevity revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyūji]] is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of [[:en:Sennyū-ji|Sennyū-ji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]]. It is held annually on the second [[:en:Monday|Monday]] of [[:en:January|January]] ([[:en:Coming of Age Day|Coming of Age Day]]) as a pilgrimage of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until [[:en:1999|1999]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]11), the event was held annually on [[:en:January 15|January 15]], similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since [[:en:1951|1951]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the [[:en:Internet Archive|Archive]])</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ([[:en:List of lucky symbols|Auspicious Items]]). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the [[:en:Sasa (plant)|Sasa]] that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q21653362}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Events in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages]] [[Category:Temples in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Temples of the Shingon Sennyu-ji branch]] [[Category:Tourism in Kyoto]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21653362]] Wikidata name: Senzan Shichifukujin Meguri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyūji}} is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward}}. It is held annually on the second {{ill|Monday|en|Monday|WD=Q105|lt=Monday}} of {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}} ({{ill|Coming of Age Day|en|Coming of Age Day|WD=Q483226|lt=Coming of Age Day}}) as a pilgrimage of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}.<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}11), the event was held annually on {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}, similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the {{ill|Internet Archive|en|Internet Archive|WD=Q461|lt=Archive}})</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ({{ill|List of lucky symbols|en|List of lucky symbols|WD=Q1532771|lt=Auspicious Items}}). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the {{ill|Sasa (plant)|en|Sasa (plant)|WD=Q2322484|lt=Sasa}} that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]]
'''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyūji}} is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward}}. It is held annually on the second {{ill|Monday|en|Monday|WD=Q105|lt=Monday}} of {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January}} ({{ill|Coming of Age Day|en|Coming of Age Day|WD=Q483226|lt=Coming of Age Day}}) as a pilgrimage of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}.<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}11), the event was held annually on {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15}}, similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the {{ill|Internet Archive|en|Internet Archive|WD=Q461|lt=Archive}})</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ({{ill|List of lucky symbols|en|List of lucky symbols|WD=Q1532771|lt=Auspicious Items}}). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the {{ill|Sasa (plant)|en|Sasa (plant)|WD=Q2322484|lt=Sasa}} that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
== List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites == {| class="wikitable" |+'''List of Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage Sites''' |- ! Number !! Sub-temple !! Deity !! Blessings !! Location |- | Site 1 || {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} || revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉山七福神巡り]]
'''Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage''' (泉山七福神巡り, せんざんしちふくじんめぐり) is an event in which pilgrims visit seven sub-temples<ref>When extras are included, the total number becomes nine. The main hall of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyūji|1=Sennyū-ji}} is also counted as an extra.</ref> located within the mountain precincts of {{ill|Sennyū-ji|en|Sennyū-ji|WD=Q1060966|lt=Sennyū-ji|1=Sennyū-ji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}. It is held annually on the second {{ill|Monday|en|Monday|WD=Q105|lt=Monday|1=Monday}} of {{ill|January|en|January|WD=Q108|lt=January|1=January}} ({{ill|Coming of Age Day|en|Coming of Age Day|WD=Q483226|lt=Coming of Age Day|1=Coming of Age Day}}) as a pilgrimage of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}.<ref>[http://www.mitera.org/7huku.php Official site of Sennyūji – Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]</ref><ref>Until {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}11), the event was held annually on {{ill|January 15|en|January 15|WD=Q2260|lt=January 15|1=January 15}}, similar to Coming of Age Day.</ref>
== Overview == The belief in the Seven Lucky Gods is said to have originated in Kyoto during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}, gradually spreading throughout Japan. It is believed that a New Year visit to the shrines brings good fortune. The Senzan Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage has been held every year on Coming of Age Day since {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}26), and it is regarded as a grand New Year tradition that grows more elaborate each year.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20161228215616/http://www.mitera.org/event.php Official Sennyūji site – Annual Events] (Archived as of December 28, 2016 from the {{ill|Internet Archive|en|Internet Archive|WD=Q461|lt=Archive|1=Internet Archive}})</ref>
On the day of the pilgrimage, participants carry the '''Fukuzasa''' – a talisman conferred at the first site, {{ill|Sokuseiin|ja|即成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokuseiin|1=Sokujō-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409172}} – to which they attach additional auspicious items ({{ill|List of lucky symbols|en|List of lucky symbols|WD=Q1532771|lt=Auspicious Items|1=List of lucky symbols}}). The Fukuzasa, made of evergreen bamboo leaves (the {{ill|Sasa (plant)|en|Sasa (plant)|WD=Q2322484|lt=Sasa|1=Sasa (plant)}} that grow vertically, symbolizing rising fortune), is held as they sequentially visit the sub-temples along {{ill|Sennyūji Road|ja|泉涌寺道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sennyūji Road|1=Sennyūji-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045328}}. The event traditionally attracts large crowds.
